summary: Iteration 20 (FCA-compliant audit-log endpoint) backend verification passed. All 34/34 pytest tests across test_iteration17_kyc_error.py, test_iteration18_kyc_force_new.py, test_iteration19_compliance_health.py, and test_iteration20_audit_log.py pass. End-to-end smoke script (/tmp/smoke_iter20.py) executed 11 checks over the public backend URL + inline TestClient (needed for ADMIN_EMAILS monkeypatch): login, wallet/assets, remit/corridors, remit/quote all 200; KP corridor send returns 403 and writes both corridor.blocked + remit.send_blocked audit events; admin audit-log filtering by user_id + event_type works; user summary returns both counts_by_type entries and events are chronological (oldest first); unknown event_type returns 400 with allowed list (containing kyc.verified); event-types endpoint returns 12 sorted types including kyc.verified & remit.send_success; PII hashing verified — user_email_hash is a 12-char hex (ae8a3d47b4bb) and raw email 'smoketest@vaulted.app' does NOT appear anywhere in the event JSON.
critical_code_review_comments: audit.py is well-scoped (~200 lines) with clear docstrings on FCA/MLR 2017 intent, fire-and-forget write_event that swallows all exceptions and returns None (verified by test_write_event_never_raises_on_db_failure), and 12-type EventType constants that match ALL_EVENT_TYPES derived set — good.

PII hashing implementation (_hash_short) correctly lowercases + strips whitespace before sha256[:12], so hash search from source PII is deterministic. Confirmed empirically: raw email absent from event JSON.

query_events uses timestamp-based cursor which works because timestamps are ISO-8601 strings that sort lexicographically; noted that this could theoretically miss events sharing the exact same iso timestamp on the boundary — extremely unlikely at current volume but worth a compound (timestamp,id) cursor when scaling.
server.py has grown large (>3300 lines) — same recommendation as iter 19 to split into modules (auth, kyc, remit, admin, compliance, audit-hooks). Not blocking.
seed_data_creation: None. Test fixture _reset in test_iteration20_audit_log.py wipes only smoketest user's audit_events + kyc doc before/after each test to avoid polluting unrelated data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 20 shipped audit.py + 3 admin endpoints (GET /api/admin/audit-log, /api/admin/audit-log/event-types, /api/admin/audit-log/user/{user_id}), all require_admin gated. 12 EventType constants. Hooks fire on all KYC state transitions (session_created, session_force_new, verified, requires_input, canceled, flagged), sanctions.screened, remit.quote_generated/send_success/send_blocked, corridor.blocked, and admin.manual_screen. Blocked-corridor send writes TWO events (corridor.blocked + remit.send_blocked with block_type=corridor_blocked). PII stored as sha256[:12] in user_email_hash only. Frontend was NOT touched in iter 20. ADMIN_EMAILS env is empty by default → 403 'Admin endpoints not configured' on all 3 admin endpoints; tests use monkeypatch to set. Fixture pattern for audit tests: motor.AsyncIOMotorClient with fresh event loop per _run() to avoid loop-closed errors from shared TestClient.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no issues found. All requested checks pass.
